en: A PSALM OF DAVID.O LORD, I call upon you; hasten to me! Give ear to my voice when I call to you!
zh: 耶和華啊！我呼求你， 求你快來幫助我； 我呼求你的時候，求你留心聽我的聲音。

en: Let my prayer be counted as incense before you, and the lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice!
zh: 願我的禱告好像香安放在你面前； 願我的手高舉好像獻晚祭。

en: Set a guard, O LORD, over my mouth; keep watch over the door of my lips!
zh: 耶和華啊！求你看守我的口， 把守我的嘴。

en: Do not let my heart incline to any evil, to busy myself with wicked deeds in company with men who work iniquity, and let me not eat of their delicacies!
zh: 求你不要容我的心偏向惡事， 免得我和作孽的人一同行惡， 也不要使我吃他們的美食。

en: Let a righteous man strike me- it is a kindness; let him rebuke me- it is oil for my head; let my head not refuse it. Yet my prayer is continually against their evil deeds.
zh: 願義人擊打我，這是出於慈愛； 願他責備我，這是膏我頭的膏油， 我的頭不會拒絕。 我仍要為惡人的惡行禱告。

en: When their judges are thrown over the cliff, then they shall hear my words, for they are pleasant.
zh: 他們的官長被摔在山崖下的時候， 他們就要聽我的話，因為我的話甘美。

en: As when one plows and breaks up the earth, so shall our bones be scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
zh: 他們說：“我們的骨頭被拋散在墓旁， 好像人犁田鋤地時掘起的土塊。”

en: But my eyes are toward you, O GOD, my Lord; in you I seek refuge; leave me not defenseless!
zh: 主耶和華啊！我的眼睛仰望你； 我投靠你，求你不要使我喪命。

en: Keep me from the trap that they have laid for me and from the snares of evildoers!
zh: 求你保護我脫離惡人為我所設的網羅， 和作孽的人的圈套。

en: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, while I pass by safely.
zh: 願惡人都落在自己的網羅裡， 我卻得以安然逃脫。
